payload.title: Ergonomics snags in inbox and law propagation routing
payload.summary: Architect observed recurring ergonomics snags while submitting a law-propagation pilot and routing follow-up CAPA envelopes: slow work-next/pending/create operations, overlarge work-next output, surprising task status projection side effects, lack of first-class law notice command, ambiguous operator-surface bindings, and unclear owned vs unowned dirty-state guidance.
evidence: inbox work-next took roughly 10-12 seconds per routing check.
task create and inbox pending paused for multiple seconds without progress indication.
inbox pending surfaced a task 1166 markdown status projection change while routing an unrelated envelope.
Law notice had to be encoded as submit-observation because no first-class law notice command exists yet.
Builder nudge delivery remains blocked by ambiguous/stale operator-surface binding state.
proposal: Create ergonomic follow-up work to make inbox work-next fast, bounded, and side-effect-clear; add first-class law notice submission; improve dirty-state ownership reporting; and repair operator-surface binding ambiguity.
payload.recommendation: Route into a focused ergonomics task or chapter rather than treating these as isolated bugs.
